title: tittmannia subulata
intro: Tittmannia subulata, also known as New Zealand forget-me-not, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant's medicinal properties include its use in traditional Māori medicine, where it was valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tittmannia subulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
